zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard FitzAlan 8th Earl of Arundel於1267年2月3日出生在英國素可阿盧德爾時，他的父親Sir John FitzAlan III20歲，母親Isabel Mortimer18歲。他於1283年與Countess Alesia di Saluzzo在英國素可結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1302年3月9日在英國英格蘭素可阿盧德爾 卡素享年35歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭史洛普爾史福斯波利。

nameMeanings.name: FitzAlan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: relationship name from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ ( see Fitz ) + the personal name Alan ( see Allen ), ‘son of Alan’.

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: Allen
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English, Old French personal name Alain, Alein (Old Breton Alan), from a Celtic personal name of great antiquity and obscurity. In England the personal name is now usually spelled Alan, the surname Allen; in Scotland the surname is more often Allan. From 1139 it was common in Scotland, where the surname also derives from Gaelic Ailéne, Ailín, from ail ‘rock’. The present-day frequency of the surname Allen in England and Ireland is partly accounted for by the popularity of the personal name among Breton followers of William the Conqueror, by whom it was imported first to Britain and then to Ireland. Saint Alan(us) was a 5th-century bishop of Quimper, who was a cult figure in medieval Brittany. Another Saint Al(l)an was a Cornish or Breton saint of the 6th century, to whom a church in Cornwall is dedicated.
English: occasionally perhaps from the rare Middle English femaje personal name Aline (Old French Adaline, Aaline), a pet form of ancient Germanic names in Adal-, especially Adalheidis (see Allis ).
French: variant of   Allain  , a cognate of 1 above, and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard FitzAlan 8th Earl of Arundel was born on 3 February 1267, in Arundel, Sussex, England, his father, Sir John FitzAlan III, was 20 and his mother, Isabel Mortimer, was 18. He married Countess Alesia di Saluzzo in 1283, in Sussex, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 9 March 1302, in Arundel Castle, Sussex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 35, and was buried in Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard FitzAlan 8th Earl of Arundel est né le 3 février 1267, à Arundel, Sussex, Angleterre, son père, Sir John FitzAlan III, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Isabel Mortimer, avait 18 ans. Il a épousé Countess Alesia di Saluzzo en 1283, à Sussex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 9 mars 1302, à Arundel Castle, Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 35 ans, et a été enterré à Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard FitzAlan 8th Earl of Arundelが1267年2月3日にイギリススセックスアルンデルで生まれた時，父Sir John FitzAlan IIIは20歳，母Isabel Mortimerは18歳であった。1283年にイギリススセックスでCountess Alesia di Saluzzoと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1302年3月9日にイギリスイギリススセックスアルンデル カストルで35歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアシュリュースブリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard FitzAlan 8th Earl of Arundel(이)가 1267년 2월 3일 영국 수세스 아런들에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John FitzAlan III(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabel Mortimer(은)는 18세였다. 그는 1283년 영국 수세스에서 Countess Alesia di Saluzzo(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1302년 3월 9일 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 아런들 카스틀에서 35세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 슈레w스버이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard FitzAlan 8th Earl of Arundel nasceu em 3 de fevereiro de 1267, em Arundel, Sussex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John FitzAlan III, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Isabel Mortimer, tinha 18 anos. Ele casou-se com Countess Alesia di Saluzzo em 1283, em Sussex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 9 de março de 1302, em Arundel Castle, Sussex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 35 anos, e foi sepultado em Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard FitzAlan 8th Earl of Arundel родился 3 февраля 1267 г. в местности арундель, суссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John FitzAlan III было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel Mortimer, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени Countess Alesia di Saluzzo в 1283 г. в местности суссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 9 марта 1302 г. в местности арундель кастл, суссекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 35 лет и был похоронен в местности шрюсбури, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания.

Se casó con Countess Alesia di Saluzzo en 1283, en Sussex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 9 de marzo de 1302, en Arundel Castle, Sussex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 35 años, y fue sepultado en Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
